parts.title: SKILL 16: LISTEN FORTWO-AND THREE-PART VERBS
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of two- and three-part verbs.

questions.passage:
(man)      The new neighbors have just moved in.
(woman)       Maybe we should call on them.
questions.question: 1.       ACCORDING TO THE WOMAN, WHAT SHOULD THEY DO?

options.text: Phone their neighbors.
Call to their neighbors over the fence .
Help the neighbors move in .
Visit their neighbors .

questions.passage:
(woman)    You know, I'm really enjoying this class now.
(man)         I am, too. At first it was kind of bor ing, but now it's turning into some thing fascinating.
questions.question: 2.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The course is becoming more interesting.
The course used to be more interesting.
The course is about the same as it was.
He's not as bored in the class as the woman.

questions.passage:
(man)      I heard you had a bad headache this morning.
(woman)       Yes, but I think I'm getting over it now.
questions.question: 3.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Her headache is getting worse.
She felt better this morning than now.
She seems to be feeling better now.
She is just getting another headache now.

questions.passage:
(man)      I'd like to stop smoking, but it's really hard for me.
(woman)       Well, at least you should try to cut down.
questions.question: 4.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man should stop breaking his cigarettes in half.
The man should decrease the number of cigarettes he smokes.
The man should cut the ends off his cigarettes.
The man should stop smoking completely.

questions.passage:
(man)      Why was the client unhappy?
(woman)       The lawyer turned down his case.
questions.question: 5.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The client presented his case to the lawyer.
The client was upset about the lawyer's rejection.
The client was annoyed because the lawyer returned the suitcase.
The client made the lawyer unhappy about the case.

questions.passage:
(woman)    I really like Marsha.
(man)         Me, too. She is just so friendly with everyone.
questions.question: 6.       WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT MARSHA?

options.text: She gets along with lots of people.
She gets back at people who cross her.
She gets rid of people she doesn't want to spend time with.
She tries to get ahead of everyone else.

questions.passage:
(woman)    Are you going out or staying here tonight?
(man)         I can't go out. I have to look after the children.
questions.question: 7.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He must try to find the children.
It is necessary for him to clean up after the children.
The children need to be watched.
He's going to see what the children have done.

questions.passage:
(woman)    Did you see in the paper this morning that one section of the factory is clos ing down today?
(man)         Yes, and some of the workers will be laid off.
questions.question: 8.       WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE WORKERS?

options.text: They are going on strike.
They are lying down on the job.
They are being released from their jobs.
They are relaxing too much at the factory.

questions.passage:
(woman)    I heard that you're trying out for the football team. Won't that be difficult?
(man)         Yes, but I'm really going to try to pull it off.
questions.question: 9.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He is betting that the football team will win.
He really wants to succeed.
It is not so difficult to play on the football team.
He pulled a muscle while playing football.

questions.passage:
(man)     I locked the keys in the car again.
(woman)       Oh, I don't know why I put up with you.
questions.question: 10.      WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's unsure why she tolerates the man.
She doesn't know where she put , her keys.
She is actually the one who put the keys in the car.
She can't understand why the man did what he did.

parts.title: SKILL 17: LISTEN FOR IDIOMS
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of idioms.

questions.passage:
(man)      I was fifteen minutes late for class today.
(woman)       Better late than never.
questions.question: 1.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man's never late.
It's good that the man was fifteen minutes late.
It's never good to be late for class.
It's good that the man went to class, on time or not.

questions.passage:
(woman)    Do you want to work on the biology experiment together?
(man)         Two heads are better than one.
questions.question: 2.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The woman's work is all in her head.
The woman has to do two experiments rather than one.
It's a good idea to work together.
The biology experiment concerns two-headed animals.

questions.passage:
(man)      I really don't want to work on the report now.
(woman)       There's no time like the present.
questions.question: 3.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She has no time to work now.
She doesn't want to work on the report either.
It's best to get it over with now.
There's no time to present the report now.

questions.passage:
(man)      I just sold the last copy of that book this morning. You'll have to try again next week.
(woman)       Just my luck.
questions.question: 4.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's very lucky to get the last book.
She's sorry she can't get the book today.
She always has good luck with books.
She just wanted to look at the book.

questions.passage:
(man)      I know you like this restaurant, but I just don't like the food here.
(woman)       To each his own.
questions.question: 5.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man doesn't like eating in restaurants.
She doesn't really like that restaurant.
Each of them has his own restaurant.
Everyone has different tastes.

questions.passage:
(man)      Would it be possible for you to drop these letters in the mailbox for me?
(woman)       No sooner said than done.
questions.question: 6.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She'll do it immediately.
It is not possible to do it.
The man should have told her sooner.
She would have done it if the man had asked.

questions.passage:
(woman)    Did you hear that Abbie won the art scholarship?
(man)         You could have knocked me down with a feather.
questions.question: 7.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Abbie used a feather in his art project.
He was knocked down.
He was really surprised.
Abbie's father knocked on the door.

questions.passage:
(man)      I have to read all six chapters this weekend.
(woman)       We're all in the same boat.
questions.question: 8.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: They are taking a boat trip together.
The six chapters are all about the boat.
Everyone has to do the same thing.
The man will read while he's on the boat.

questions.passage:
(woman)    Anne's project for the science fair was incredible.
(man)         She's really head and shoulders above the rest.
questions.question: 9.       WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT ANNE?

options.text: She is taller than the others.
She put her science project on top of the others.
She has a really good head on her shoulders.
She's the best of them all.

questions.passage:
(man)     I can't believe how many forms I have to fill out to apply for the scholarship.
(woman)       Yes, and you should be sure to dot all the i's and cross all the t's.
questions.question: 10.      WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man needs to improve his penmanship.
The man doesn't really need to apply for the scholarship.
The man needs to fill out the application with dots and crosses.
The man needs to pay attention to every detail.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you do the cooking every night?
(woman) No, my roommate and I take turns.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She gets lots of take-out dinners.
She and her roommate alternate cooking responsibilities.
Her roommate cooks more often than she does.
Her roommate does the cooking while she does other chores.

questions.passage:
(man) Did you meet Hank's father at the game last night?
(woman) Yes, I did. You know, Hank's really a chip off the old block.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT HANK?

options.text: He resembles his father.
He has a chipped tooth.
He lives one block from his father.
He and his father were playing a game with blocks.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you want to go to the football game with us tonight?
(woman) No, thanks. I think I'm coming down with something.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's going somewhere else.
She does not like football.
She has a lot of work to do.
She is getting sick.

questions.passage:
(woman) I can't believe what Hal said to the teacher!
(man)  Yeah, he really put his foot in his mouth.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT HAL?

options.text: He put his foot where he should not have.
He put the food that the teacher gave him into his mouth.
He said something embarrassing.
He told the teacher that his foot was hurt.

questions.passage:
(man) I'm going to the market now.
(woman) Could you hold off going for a few minutes? I'd like to go with you.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She'd like the man to delay his trip.
She prefers that the man leave a few minutes earlier than he planned.
She wants to know if the man will stay in the market for only a few minutes.
She'd like to talk to the man for a few minutes.

questions.passage:
(man) I got a parking ticket, but I don't think I want to pay it.
(woman) You know, you're really playing with fire.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man might start a fire in the park.
The man parked his car near the fire.
The man's thinking of doing something dangerous.
The man's playing a game in the park.

questions.passage:
(man) The noise from those machines is really bothering me.
(woman) Yeah, I wish we could just ask them to cut it out.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The machines do not act very well.
The machines don't really bother her.
She would like them to stop the noise.
She wishes the machines would cut the wood.

questions.passage:
(man) Did Fred tell you his theory about who took the money?
(woman) Yes, and I really think he's barking up the wrong tree.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Fred has a dog that barks a lot.
Fred has hidden the money in a tree.
Fred has backed into a tree.
Fred has made a mistake.

questions.passage:
(man) This course is too hard for me. I think
(woman) I'll just drop it.
questions.question: 9. Even though it's hard, you should keep at it as long as you can. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN TELL THE MAN?

options.text: She will give him any help he needs.
He has to give away what he doesn't need.
He should not give up.
He should give back what he borrowed.

questions.passage:
(man) How's the chemistry homework com ing?
(woman) It's like trying to swim upstream.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She'd rather go swimming than do the homework.
The chemistry homework is really difficult.
She's doing the homework by the swimming pool.
The stream is drying up.

parts.title: Skills 01-17
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Could you put some more wood in the fireplace?
(man)         I'll have to bring some in from out side.
questions.question: 1.       WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: There's no more wood inside.
The wood in the fireplace should be put outside.
There's a fire outside.
He needs to bring some wood outside.

questions.passage:
(man)         Alice, I thought you were working late tonight.
(woman)       I was supposed to, but my conference was called off.
questions.question: 2.       WHAT HAPPENED TO ALICE?

options.text: She worked late at a conference.
Her meeting was canceled.
She called a conference at work.
She was late to a conference.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Why are you getting out of the water?
(man)         The lifeguard motioned that we should move in that direction.
questions.question: 3.       WHERE DOES THE CONVERSATION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: SHORT DIALOGUES (Paper and Computer) In a hospital .
At a police station .
At the beach .
In a locker room .

questions.passage:
(woman)       Did you enjoy the evening at the nightclub?
(man)         I wish there had been a little more room on the dance floor.
questions.question: 4.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: There was too much room on the dance floor.
He enjoyed the room where they went dancing.
The dance floor was too crowded.
The club needed more rooms for dancing.

questions.passage:
(man)         I just sent off the letter that you wrote.
(woman)       Then, you did figure out how to use the fax machine.
questions.question: 5.       WHAT HAD THE WOMAN ASSUMED ABOUT THE MAN?

options.text: He could not understand the fax machine.
He wrote the letter that was sent.
The fax machine was easy for him to use.
He was not very good with figures.

questions.passage:
(woman)       I guessed that you would buy that new car.
(man)         And you hit the nail on the head.
questions.question: 6.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The woman hit her head on a nail.
The woman hit his new car.
The woman was exactly right.
The woman bought the new car.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Is that your research paper for Eng lish Lit.?
(man)         Yes, and it's almost finished. Do you think you could look it over for me?
questions.question: 7.       WHAT DOES THE MAN WANT?

options.text: He would like the woman to help him find his paper.
He wants the woman to put the paper away.
He needs the woman to review the paper.
He would like the woman to write the paper for him.

questions.passage:
(man)         Has everyone been informed?
(woman)       No one is unaware of the situation.
questions.question: 8.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Information about the problem is unavailable.
No one has been informed.
Everybody knows what is going on.
Nobody is aware that the problem is serious.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Did you sleep well last night?
(man)         If the alarm were not so loud, I would never have woken up.
questions.question: 9.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He did not sleep well.
He never woke up this morning.
The alarm failed to go off.
He needed a loud alarm to wake up.

questions.passage:
(woman)       I could not believe the story in the news about the problem with the air plane.
(man)         Yeah, I saw it, too! The pilot was forced to land the plane in a field.
questions.question: 10.      WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The pilot made an emergency landing.
The pilot was forced to leave the plane in a hurry.
The pilot fielded questions about the forced landing.
The plane was damaged when it landed forcefully.
